q: The initial idea to preserve the Glorious Qur'an was mooted by
explanation: After the demise of the Prophet(p), the first Caliph Abu Bakr, assembled all the Huffaz (those who have committed the Quran to memory), and the written records of the Glorious Quran and with their help had the whole text written in Book form.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Q. 4:34 enjoins the wife not only to be obedient but also to
explanation: Q. 4:34 enjoins the wife not only to be obedient but also to maintain her chastity.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Talaq al ba in means divorce that is
explanation: This type of talaq occurs when nikah is completely annulled and the couple cannot live together anymore. If husband and wife both agree to get back together, they must renew their nikah which means they have to performed their nikah again. This type of divorce is known as talaqul ba'in.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: By the year 1110 C.E., Andalusia had fallen under the control of
explanation: By the year 1110 C.E., Andalusia had fallen under the control of Al Murabitun.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the early part of Islam in West Africa, the ribat was primarily a centre for
explanation: Ribat was originally used as a term to describe a frontier post where travelers (particularly soldiers) could stay to promote Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The minimum amount that may be stolen to warrant cutting of the hand is
explanation: The minimum amount that may be stolen to warrant cutting of the hand is 1/4 Dinar.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Forbidden Munkar in Islam means discouraging others from
explanation: Munkar is one name that covers everything banned by Allah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the contribution of Islam to women welfare is the introduction of
explanation: One of the contribution of Islam to women welfare is the introduction of inheritance. In Islam, women are entitled the right of inheritance, though generally, Islam allots women half the share of inheritance available to men if they inherit from the same father. For example, where the decedent has both male and female children, a son's share is double that of a daughter's.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What are the characteristics common to most Makkan Suwar?
explanation: Most Makkan Suwar are short and poetic.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Prophet Muhammad's (SAW) answer to Angel Jibril when he was asked to recite was
explanation: The Prophet Muhammad (SAW) response to Jibril was that He cannot read.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Divine inspiration was employed in the
explanation: The arrangement of the Glorious Qur'an was divinely inspired.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Among the obligatory steps of ablution are intention, washing the face, washing the hands to the elbow and also
explanation: Upon washing hands and elbow, the next is to wash the feet.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Imam Malik was born in
explanation: Imam Malik was born in Medina in 711 CE.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Arabs worshipped idols during the Jahiliyya period because
options: They were ignorant of God's existence
explanation: They were ignorant of God's existence

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The gradual spiritual and moral development of Muslims was the wisdom behind the
options: piecemeal revelation of the Glorious Qur'an
explanation: The premise or the basis for the piecemeal or bit by bit revelation of the Qur'an was the gradual spiritual and moral development of Muslims

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The correct meaning of Tafsir is the
options: detailed explanation and commentary of the Glorious Qur'an
scientific application of the contents of the Glorious Qur'an to human life
true reflections on the contents of the Glorious Qur'an
practical application of the contents of the Glorious Qur'an
explanation: Tafsir is the science of explanation of the Qurʾān, the sacred scripture of Islam, or of Qurʾānic commentary.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Seeking assistance from the fortune tellers is forbidden because
options: it gives one an insight of what tomorrow holds
it creates animosity amongst the people
it leads to ascribing absolute power to a fellow being
explanation: Seeking assistance from the fortune tellers is forbidden because it leads to ascribing absolute power to a fellow being

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A father or guardian can give out his daughter or ward in marriage under the principle of Ijbar provided that the
options: girl has attained the age of maturity
groom does not have any physical disability
explanation: A father or guardian can give out his daughter or ward in marriage under the principle of Ijbar provided that the girl is a minor. Under Islamic law, a wali mujbir has an authority to contract the marriage of his virgin daughter even though she does not agree with the marriage. The authority is known as wilayat al-ijbar and quite often described as marriage by compulsion.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Sadaq is the exclusive property of the
explanation: The Dower (Sadaq) is a sum of money or other property which becomes. payable by the husband to the wife as an effect of marriage. The Quran ordains: "And give the women ( on marriage) their dower as a free gift." ( 4:4) In another. Hence it is the property of the wife.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the following played a major role in the transmission of Hadiths?
explanation: A'ishah, the wife of Prophet Muhammad, played a significant role in the transmission of Hadiths. She narrated over 2000 hadiths, making her one of the most important narrators of Hadiths.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The supererogatory prayer with a single rak'ah is Salatul
explanation: Salatul Witr is a supererogatory prayer that is performed with a single rak'ah. It is usually performed after the Isha prayer and before the Fajr prayer.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are voluntary fasting except
explanation: Qada' is not a voluntary fast. It is the fast that is made up for a missed obligatory fast. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Qada'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Voluntary fasting is encouraged on
explanation: Voluntary fasting is encouraged on Mondays and Thursdays in Islam. This is based on Hadiths from Prophet Muhammad (PBUH). Therefore, the correct answer is 'Mondays'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first King of the Mali empire to accept Islam was
explanation: He was the first Muslim ruler in West Africa to make the journey to Mecca.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Permanent exemption from Ramadan fast may be occasioned by
explanation: Permanent exemption from Ramadan fast may be occasioned by old age. According to Islamic jurisprudence, elderly people who find it extremely difficult to fast are allowed to break their fast and instead feed a poor person for each day they do not fast.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Quran 24:32 is an injunction which
options: allows a Muslim to divorce only when necessary
commands Muslims to marry more than one wife
enjoins Muslims to marry righteous partners
explanation: Quran 24:32 is an injunction which enjoins Muslims to marry righteous partners. The verse says: 'And marry the unmarried among you and the righteous among your male slaves and female slaves. If they should be poor, Allah will enrich them from His bounty, and Allah is all-Encompassing and Knowing.' It neither discourages a Muslim from polygamy, commands Muslims to marry more than one wife, nor allows a Muslim to divorce only when necessary.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The goddesses of the Jahiliyyah Arabs include
explanation: The goddesses of the Jahiliyyah Arabs include al-Lat, al-Manat, and al-Uzzah. However, as-samad is not a goddess of the Jahiliyyah Arabs. As-Samad is one of the names of Allah, mentioned in Surah Al-Ikhlas.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is not a form of shirk according to the Quran?
options: Which of the following is not a form of shirk according to the Quran?
explanation: Monotheism, the belief in one God, is not a form of shirk according to the Quran. Shirk, in Islam, is the sin of practicing idolatry or polytheism, i.e., the deification or worship of anyone or anything other than the singular God. Therefore, Trinity (belief in Father, Son, and Holy Spirit as three persons in one God), Polytheism (belief in many gods), and Atheism (disbelief in the existence of God or gods) can be considered forms of shirk.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: When Muhammad(S.A.W) sought for protection in Ta'if in 620 C.E. he was
options: stoned by the people of Ta'if
allowed to stay for a few days
explanation: When Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) sought protection in Ta'if in 620 C.E., he was stoned and chased out of the city by the people of Ta'if. This event is known as the 'Year of Sorrow' in Islamic history.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The reign of Caliph Uthman b. Affan is known for
explanation: The reign of Caliph Uthman b. Affan is known for the standardization of the Qur'an. He commissioned a committee to produce a standard copy of the text, which is known as the Uthmanic codex.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: How many times does the word al-Qadr occur in the Qur'an chapter 97?
explanation: The word al-Qadr occurs 3 times in the Qur'an chapter 97. Al-Qadr is an Arabic word that means 'The Night of Decree' or 'The Night of Power'. It is a special night in the month of Ramadan when the first verses of the Quran were revealed to the Prophet Muhammad.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The capital of an ancient Ghana empire was
explanation: The capital of the ancient Ghana Empire was Awkar. The Ghana Empire was a West African empire located in the area of present-day southeastern Mauritania and western Mali. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Awkar'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to the Hadith of the Prophet(S.A.W.), learning is obligatory for both the
explanation: According to a Hadith of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.), 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim, male and female.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'Male and Female'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: "Lam yalid wa lam yulad"(Q 112:3) is preceded by
explanation: "Lam yalid wa lam yulad"(Q 112:3) is preceded by "Allahu Samad"(Q 112:2). This is a verse from the Quran, Surah Al-Ikhlas. The verse "Lam yalid wa lam yulad" translates to "He begets not, nor is He begotten" and the preceding verse "Allahu Samad" translates to "Allah, the Eternal Refuge".

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratud-Duha (Q.93) in the Quran has a total of 11 verses. Therefore, without the last verse, the remaining verses are 10.
explanation: Suratud-Duha (Q.93) in the Quran has a total of 11 verses. Therefore, without the last verse, the remaining verses are 10.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the science of Hadith, the next Sahih to authenticity is
explanation: In the science of Hadith, the classification of Hadiths in terms of authenticity is as follows: Sahih (Authentic), Hasan (Good), and Da'if (Weak). Therefore, the next to Sahih in terms of authenticity is Hasan.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Imam Bukhari is to 870 C.E. as Imam Muslim is to
explanation: Imam Muslim, a prominent scholar of Hadith, was born in 817 CE and died in 875 CE. However, his most significant work, Sahih Muslim, was completed in 888 CE.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The number of Rakah in the obligatory prayers of Friday is
explanation:
Fajr = 2
Jummah = 2
Asr = 4
Magrib = 3
Ishai = 4

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is the greatest form of Jihad according to the Prophet(S.A.W.)?
options: waging war in the course of A llah
explanation: The greatest form of Jihad according to the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) is suppressing one's selfish desires. This is often referred to as the 'greater jihad', the struggle against the lower self (nafs). The 'lesser jihad' is the physical struggle against the enemies of Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A major lesson contained in Hadith 16 of an-Nawawi is
explanation: Hadith 16 of an-Nawawi is about controlling anger. The Hadith says: 'I asked the Messenger of Allah: Who is the best among the people? He replied, 'The one who, when you look at him, you remember Allah.' Then I asked: Then who? He replied, 'The one who does not harm the Muslims with his tongue and hands.' Then I asked: Then who? He replied, 'The one who controls his anger.'

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Makkah is referred to in the Quran chapter 95 verse 3 as the city of
explanation: In the Quran chapter 95 verse 3, Makkah is referred to as the city of security. The verse says 'And [by] this secure city [Makkah],' Therefore, the correct answer is 'security'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Tahajjud is observed between
explanation: The Tahajjud prayer is observed between Isha (night prayer) and Fajr (dawn prayer). It is a voluntary prayer and is highly recommended in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following Suwar begin with divine command to the Prophet except Surah
explanation: Surah ad-Duha does not begin with a divine command to the Prophet. It begins with an oath by the morning light (Duha) and the night when it settles. The other Surahs listed (an-Nas, al-Falaq, al-Kafirun) all begin with a command or a statement to the Prophet.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A Juz' of the Quran is subdivided into the following except
explanation: A Juz' of the Quran is subdivided into Hizb, Nisf, and Rub'. Manzil is not a subdivision of a Juz'. Instead, the Quran is divided into seven Manzil to allow for it to be read over a week.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: "La Taghdab" in Hadith 16 means
explanation: "La Taghdab" in Hadith 16 means "do not get angry". This is a part of a Hadith where a man asked the Prophet for advice and he replied, "Do not get angry".

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Surah which beins with four oaths is
explanation: The Surah which begins with four oaths is Surah at-Tin (The Fig). The Surah starts with the verses "By the fig and the olive, And [by] Mount Sinai, And [by] this secure city [Makkah]," which are considered as oaths.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Imam an-Nasal spent his days in
explanation: Imam an-Nasai, a notable Hadith scholar, spent his days in Khurasan. He was born in the city of Nasa in present-day Turkmenistan, and he spent most of his life studying and teaching Hadith in the region of Khurasan.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is an exclusive obligation of the husband?
explanation: In Islam, the provision of shelter is an exclusive obligation of the husband. This is part of the financial responsibilities of the husband towards his wife, which also includes providing food and clothing. Counselling, provision of job, and domestic support are not exclusive obligations of the husband.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The last verse of Suratut-Tin describes Allah as the
explanation: The last verse of Suratut-Tin describes Allah as the 'Judge'. The verse is 'Is not Allah the most just of judges?' (Surah At-Tin, 95:8). Therefore, the correct answer is 'Judge'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Muslims should pay more attention to Hadith 5 of an-Nawawi because it
explanation: It discourages innovation in religion.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The major teaching of Hadith 5 of an-Nawawi centres on
explanation: On the authority of Aishah, who said: The messenger of Allah said: p "He who innovates something in this matter of ours that is not of it will have it rejected." And in one version by Muslim it reads : "He who does an act which our matter is not in agreement with will have it rejected.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: To seek divine guidance, Muslims are encouraged to perform Salat
explanation: Salat Istikhara is a prayer for seeking divine guidance in decision-making. It is recommended when a Muslim is making a decision and seeks the guidance of Allah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Istikhara'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul Fatihah(Q.1:5) admonishes
options: appreciate the mercy and grace of Allah
give thanks and praises to Allah
explanation: Suratul Fatihah (Q.1:5) is the first chapter of the Quran and it emphasizes the worship of Allah alone. The verse says 'You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'Worship none except Almighty Allah'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A muslim is only permitted to marry his
explanation: According to Islamic law, a Muslim man is permitted to marry his late wife's sister. However, he is not permitted to marry his foster mother, his late father's wife, or his foster sister.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The state into which the pilgrim enters befire the time of Hajj is
explanation: The state into which the pilgrim enters before the time of Hajj is called Ihram. Ihram is a sacred state which a Muslim must enter in order to perform the major pilgrimage (Hajj) or the minor pilgrimage (Umrah).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Halimah, the wet nurse of Muhammad(S.A.W.) hailed from the Banu
explanation: Halimah, the wet nurse of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.), hailed from the Banu Sa'ad tribe. This tribe was known for its generosity and hospitality.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The major teaching of Surah an-Nasr(Q. 110) is that
options: man must strive to succeed in life
explanation: Surah an-Nasr (Q. 110) discusses the victory of Islam and the rapid expansion of the Muslim community. Therefore, the major teaching of this Surah is that Islam has a bright future.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet lost his wife Khadijah in the year
explanation: The Prophet Muhammad lost his wife Khadijah in the year 619 C.E. This year is also known as the 'Year of Sorrow' in Islamic history due to the passing of both Khadijah and Muhammad's uncle, Abu Talib.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The weakest of faith according to Hadith 34 of an-Nawawi is changig an evil with the
explanation: According to Hadith 34 of an-Nawawi, the weakest form of faith is changing an evil with the heart, which means hating it and feeling that it is wrong. This is considered the least that can be done when encountering evil.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first Mu'adh-Dhin in Islam is
explanation: The first Mu'adh-Dhin in Islam is Bilal. Bilal ibn Rabah was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad and the first man to call Muslims to prayer, known as the Adhan, making him the first Mu'adh-Dhin in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following was not a Jahilliyyah idol?
explanation: The Jahilliyyah period refers to the time of ignorance before the advent of Islam in Arabia. During this period, the Arabs worshipped several idols, including al-Manat, al-Uzzah, and al-Lat. However, al-Jibl is not known to be one of the Jahilliyyah idols.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The work of Imam Bukhari and al-Tirmidhi are both called
explanation: The works of Imam Bukhari and al-Tirmidhi are both called 'Jami'. Jami is a term used for a collection of hadiths, which are sayings, actions or approvals of the Prophet Muhammad.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Alaysal-Llahu biahkamil-Hakimin implies that Allahi is the best
explanation: The phrase 'Alaysal-Llahu biahkamil-Hakimin' is from the Quran (95:8) and it translates to 'Is not Allah the most just of judges?'. Therefore, it implies that Allah is the best Judge.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are lessons of Hadith 5 of an-Nawawi except
explanation: On the authority of Aishah, who said : The messenger of Allah said: p "He who innovates something in this matter of ours that is not of it will have it rejected."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The admonition of the last part of Hadith 18 of an-Nawawi is
explanation: Have taqwa (fear) of Allah wherever you may be, and follow up a bad deed with a good deed which will wipe it out, and behave well towards the people.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following nullifies an Islamic marriage if it is not fulfilled except
explanation: The following nullifies an Islamic marriage if it is not fulfilled except marriage feast. In Islamic law, the marriage feast (Walima) is a Sunnah (practice of the Prophet) and not a requirement for the validity of the marriage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The number of Makkan soldiers that took part in the battle of Uhud was
explanation: The Battle of Uhud was a significant battle in early Islamic history, taking place in 625 C.E. The Makkan army that fought against the Muslims in this battle was made up of approximately 3000 soldiers.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Maliki yawmid-Din is translated as
options: guide us through the right path
praise be to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds
the King of the Day of Judgement
explanation: 'Maliki yawmid-Din' is a phrase from the Quran which translates to 'the King of the Day of Judgement'. It is part of the first chapter of the Quran, Al-Fatiha.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ad-Dhikr is to the Qur'an as Ummul-Kitab is to
explanation: Ad-Dhikr is another name for the Qur'an, and Ummul-Kitab (Mother of the Book) is another name for Surah Al-Fatihah. Therefore, Ad-Dhikr is to the Qur'an as Ummul-Kitab is to Fatihah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Subhana rabbiyal -A'la faghfir li is recited when the worshipper is at
explanation: The phrase 'Subhana rabbiyal -A'la faghfir li' is recited during the act of sujud (prostration) in the Islamic prayer. Therefore, the correct answer is 'sujud'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are classifications of Hadith except
explanation: The term 'hasam' is not a classification of Hadith. The Hadiths are classified into 'sahih' (authentic), 'daif' (weak), and 'mawdu' (fabricated).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ijma' as a secondary source of Islamic law implies
explanation: Ijma' is an Arabic term referring generally to the consensus or agreement of the Muslim scholars basically on religious issues. It is considered the third fundamental source of Islamic law, after the Qur'an and Sunnah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Tayammum is performed by a Muslim when
options: he wants to join a congregational prayer
access to water is very difficult
he is in a hurry to perform prayer
explanation: Tayammum is a dry ablution in Islamic ritual purification for prayer, which involves the use of clean soil or dust when water is not available or cannot be used for reasons such as illness. Therefore, the correct answer is 'access to water is very difficult'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A complete prayer ends with
explanation: A complete Islamic prayer (Salah) ends with Taslim. Taslim is the part where the person praying turns their head to the right and then to the left, saying 'Assalamu alaikum wa rahmatullah' each time, which means 'Peace be upon you and God's mercy'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first pledge of Aqabah was signed in
explanation: The first pledge of Aqabah, also known as the First Pledge of Allegiance at al-Aqabah, was an agreement between the Islamic prophet Muhammad and twelve men from Yathrib (now Medina). This event took place in 621 C.E.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The unity of Allah is expressed in Suratul -
explanation: The unity of Allah is expressed in Suratul Ikhlas. This Surah (chapter) of the Quran is often translated as 'The Sincerity' and it emphasizes the oneness and singularity of Allah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet's uncle who succeeded Abu Talib as the Head of the Banu Hashum was
explanation: The Prophet's uncle who succeeded Abu Talib as the Head of the Banu Hashum was Hamzah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Qur'an was initially preserved on the following except
explanation: The Qur'an was initially preserved through memory, on leather, and on rocks. Paper was not used for the initial preservation of the Qur'an.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Lawhul-Mahfudh is associated with Quranic
explanation: Lawhul-Mahfudh is associated with Quranic preservation. It is believed to be the 'Preserved Tablet' in the heavens where everything that has happened and will happen is recorded. This concept is related to the divine predestination and preservation of the Quran.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Marriage relationship fulfils the following functions except
explanation: Marriage in Islam serves several purposes, including sexual satisfaction, forming the foundation of family life, and providing security in old age. However, it is not considered a quick means of getting rich. Therefore, the statement 'quick means of getting rich' is not a function of marriage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Khadijah married Muhammad(S.A.W.) at the age of
explanation: Khadijah married Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) when she was 40 years old. This is a well-known fact in Islamic history.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The last child of Prophet Muhammad(S.A.W) is
explanation: The last child of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) was Ibrahim, who was born to Maria al-Qibtiyya. He lived for about 16 to 18 months and then died.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Qur'an is to Muhammad(S.A.W.) as Suhuf is to
explanation: The Qur'an was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.), and the Suhuf (Scrolls) were revealed to Prophet Ibrahim (A.S). Therefore, the Qur'an is to Muhammad (S.A.W.) as Suhuf is to Ibrahim (A.S).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: An Hadith is referred to as da'if because
options: it has defects in its isnad and matn
it is sometimes supported with evidences
explanation: A Hadith is referred to as da'if (weak) because it has defects in its isnad (chain of narrators) and matn (content). This means that there may be issues with the reliability of the narrators or inconsistencies in the content of the Hadith.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Talaq al-Bida'ah takes effect when divorce is pronounced
options: when the wife is still in her marital home
when the wife is purified of her menses
explanation: Talaq al-Bida'ah, also known as the innovated divorce, takes effect when divorce is pronounced three times simultaneously. This practice is considered to be against the teachings of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) as it does not allow for the period of reconciliation that is recommended in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following prayers is not observed between Isha and Fajr?
explanation: Istisqa is a prayer for rain in the Islamic tradition. It is not observed between Isha and Fajr. On the other hand, Tahajjud, Shafi, and Witr are prayers that are observed during this period.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The fifty Muslim archers at the battle of Uhud were instructed to
options: strike first in order to gain war booty
attack the enemies when facing them
stay there and should not leave their post
explanation: The fifty Muslim archers at the battle of Uhud were instructed by Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) to stay at their post and not to leave, regardless of the situation on the battlefield. Their failure to adhere to this instruction led to the Muslims' defeat in the battle.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al-Akhirah is to hereafter as Al-Qiyammah is to
explanation: In Islamic eschatology, Al-Akhirah is the term for the afterlife, and Al-Qiyammah is the term for the Day of Resurrection. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Resurrection'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The type of Ghusl performed by a ew convert to Islam is
explanation: When a person converts to Islam, they are required to perform Ghusl (ritual purification) known as Ghusl al-Islam or Dhukil-al-Islam. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Dhukil-al-Islam'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A rak'ah in salat starts with
explanation: A rak'ah in salat starts with Takbiratul Ihram. Takbiratul Ihram is the first Takbir (saying 'Allahu Akbar') in the prayer which marks the beginning of the prayer.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the issues discussed in Hadith 18 of an-Nawawi is about
explanation: Have taqwa (fear) of Allah wherever you may be, and follow up a bad deed with a good deed which will wipe it out, and behave well towards the people. It was related by at-Tirmidhi, who said it was a hasan (good) hadeeth, and in some copies it is stated to be a hasan saheeh hadeeth.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Reference is made to the migration of 622 C.E. in an-Nawawi's Hadith
explanation: The migration referred to in an-Nawawi's Hadith is the Hijrah, which took place in 622 C.E. This was when Prophet Muhammad and his followers migrated from Mecca to Medina. The Hijrah marks the beginning of the Islamic calendar, hence it is often referred to in Islamic literature.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is not among the articles of Faith? Belief in the
explanation: The articles of Faith in Islam include belief in the angels, prophets, and scriptures. However, belief in miracles is not considered an article of Faith. While miracles are recognized in Islam, they are not a separate category of belief.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Iqra' warabbukal' akram is followed by
explanation: The phrase 'Iqra' warabbukal' akram' is from the first revelation of the Quran to Prophet Muhammad. It is followed by 'Alladhi' alama bil qalam', which translates to 'Who taught by the pen'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The main means of transport used by the Arabs in the desert was
explanation: During the pre-Islamic period, the main means of transport used by the Arabs in the desert was the camel. Camels are well-adapted to the harsh desert conditions and were used for long-distance travel, carrying heavy loads, and even as a source of food and milk.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul fatihah is recited ____ times in suratul asr
explanation: Suratul Fattihah, the first chapter of the Qur'an is recited four times during the Asr prayer.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Iblis was created from
explanation: Iblis was created from fire. According to Islamic belief, Iblis (Satan) was created from fire and was once a pious worshipper, but he became arrogant and disobedient, leading to his downfall.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the following attempted to destroy the ka'abah?
explanation: Abrahah, the Ethiopian general, led a military campaign to destroy the Ka'bah in the 6th century CE.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following prayers has silent recitation?
explanation: During the Zuhr, the Imam recites certain prayers silently, while the congregation follows in silence.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following vitiates ablution except
explanation: Falling into a deep sleep does not invalidate ablution, unlike other actions like eating or drinking.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who is not among the khulafa 'ul Rashidun except?
explanation: Mu'awiyah, a companion of the Prophet and later a caliph, is not considered one of the Khulafa'ul-Rashidun, who are specifically the first four caliphs mentioned above.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Twenty prophets were mentioned in the Qur'an.
explanation: The Qur'an mentions the stories and teachings of twenty-five prophets, including Adam, Ibrahim, Musa, Isa, and Muhammad.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are acts of hajj except
explanation: Ziyarah refers to visiting the Prophet's Mosque in Madinah, which is not an essential act of Hajj (pilgrimage to Makkah).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Khulafa'ul-Rashidun are in number.
explanation: The Khulafa'ul-Rashidun, the Rightly-Guided Caliphs, are four in number: Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A person who worships other beings is called
explanation: Idolatry is the worship of idols, gods, or goddesses besides Allah, which is considered a fundamental violation o the Islamic concept of Tawhid (the oneness of God).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Sand ablution is known as al
explanation: Tayammum is the process of performing ablution using sand or dust when water is not available.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Salatul-tarawihta rawih is observed in the month of
explanation: Salatul-tarawih is a special congregational prayer performed in the evening during the month of Ramadan.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The pilgrims go to arafah on the day of Dhul-Hijjah
explanation: During the Hajj pilgrimage, Muslims gather at the plain of Arafah on the ninth day of Dhul-Hijjah, where they perform the most essential ritual of Hajj.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Subhana rabbiyal a'la wabihamdihi is recited in
explanation: This phrase is recited during the Ruku' position in prayer, which involves bowing down and glorifying Allah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A muslim can marry the following except
explanation: In Islamic law, marriage between close relatives, including siblings, is prohibited.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are sources of pure water except
explanation: Sewage water is considered impure and unfit for use in Islamic rituals.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Salatul Eidayn has______ raka'ah each.
explanation: The Eid prayers (Salatul Eidayn) consist of two units (raka'at) each.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Eid al-Adha is observed on the day of dhul-Hijjah
explanation: Eid al-Adha, also known as the Feast of Sacrifice, is celebrated on the tenth day of Dhul-Hijjah, marking the end of the Hajj pilgrimage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Angels were created from
explanation: In Islamic belief, angels are created from light and are divine messengers, serving as intermediaries between Allah and humanity.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are obligatory acts of Hajj except
explanation: Tawaf is the circumambulation of the Ka'bah, and it is an essential ritual during the Hajj pilgrimage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is not among the Ulul Azim? Prophet
explanation: Ulul Azim refers to the five most prominent prophets in Islam: Muhammad, Ibrahim, Musa, Nuh, and Idris. Whil Isa is a significant prophet in Islam, he is not included in this specific group.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ramadan is the ____ month of hijrah calendar.
explanation: Ramadan is the ninth month of the Islamic lunar calendar and is considered a sacred month, during which Muslims fast from dawn to sunset.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ghuslul is performed after the stoppage of menstrual blood.
explanation: In Islamic jurisprudence, Ghuslul Janabah is the ritual bath performed after sexual intercourse, menstruation, or childbirth.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The prayer that has three raka'at is salatul
explanation: The Maghrib prayer, performed just after sunset, consists of three units (raka'at).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The angels responsible are recording of deeds are ____ and ____
explanation: In Islamic belief, Raqib and Atid are the two angels responsible for recording an individual's good and bad deeds

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Salatul istisqa is prayer for
explanation: Salatul istisqa is a special prayer performed to seek rain and relief from drought.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Tahara simply means
explanation: In Islamic terminology, Tahara refers to the process of purification, including ablution (wudu) and bathing (ghusl).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al-Rahim means the
explanation: Al-Rahim is one of the names of Allah, emphasizing His mercy and forgiveness.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are articles of faith except belief in
explanation: The following are articles of faith except belief in Imam (d). In Islam, the articles of faith include belief in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, and the Day of Judgment. Belief in an Imam (leader) is not considered an essential article of faith.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Subhana Rabbiyal azim wabihamdih is recited in
explanation: This phrase is recited during the Ruku position in prayer, which involves bowing down and glorifying Allah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al-Rahman means the
explanation: Al-Rahman is another name of Allah, highlighting His compassion and mercy towards all creation.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Ulul Azim are in number
explanation: The Ulul Azim are five in number (b). As mentioned earlier, the five most prominent prophets in Islam are Muhammad, Ibrahim, Musa, Nuh, and Idris.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Washing the hands to the elbow is ___ in ablution.
explanation: Washing hands up to the elbows is considered a recommended act (sunnah) during ablution.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the following attempted to destroy the ka'abah?
explanation: Abrahah, the Ethiopian general, led a military campaign to destroy the Ka'bah in the 6th century CE.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Zuhr prayer has_____ raka'at.
explanation: The Zuhr prayer, performed at noon, consists of four units (raka'at).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The pilgrims go to arafah on the ___ day of Dhul-Hijjah
explanation: During the Hajj pilgrimage, Muslims gather at the plain of Arafah on the ninth day of Dhul-Hijjah, where they perform the most essential ritual of Hajj.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ka'abah was by Prophet Ibrahim (AS).
explanation: According to Islamic tradition, the Ka'bah was built by Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) and his son Ismail (Ishmael)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the following angels is in charge of rain
explanation: In Islamic tradition, Mika'il is responsible for bringing rain and provisions to Earth.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are acts of hajj except
explanation: Ziyarah refers to visiting the Prophet's Mosque in Madinah, which is not an essential act of Hajj (pilgrimage to Makkah).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al-khaliq means the
explanation: Al-Khaliq is one of the names of Allah, emphasizing His role as the creator of the universe and all that exists.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The word Talaq means
explanation: In Islamic terminology, Talaq refers to the process of divorce or dissolution of marriage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following acts are prohibited while in ihram except
explanation: During the state of Ihram (consecration) for Hajj or Umrah, certain activities are prohibited, but bathing is not one of them.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al-Qur'an is a divine book from
explanation:
The Quran is the holy book of Islam and is considered a divine revelation from Allah (God) to humanity. Muslim believe that the Quran is the literal word of God as revealed to the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) ove a period of 23 years. It is a guide for living a righteous life, and is considered the ultimate source of guidance anc wisdom for Muslims.
While angels, jinn, messengers, and prophets all play important roles in Islamic theology, the Quran is ultimately considered a divine book from Allah Himself.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of these hadith narrated the dialogue between the prophet (SAW) and angel Jibril?
explanation: Hadith 1 of An-Nawawi's collections narrates the dialogue between the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and angel Jibril, in which Jibril asks the Prophet to explain the meaning of Islam, Iman, and Ihsan. This Hadith is known as the "Hadith of Gabriel" and is considered a fundamental text in understanding the basics of Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The word al-Bayt in suratul Quraysh refers to the
explanation:
The word "al-Bayt" in suratul Quraysh refers to :
- Ka'abah: The word "al-Bayt" in suratul Quraysh refers to the Ka'bah in Makkah. This is mentioned in verse 3, which says, "Let them worship the Lord of this House." This house refers to the Ka'bah, which is a center of pilgrimage and a significant part of the life of the Quraysh tribe.
Other meanings of "al-Bayt" include:
- People of the House: "Ahl al-Bayt" is a term used to refer to the family of the Islamic prophet Muhammad. - Household: In the absence of the definite article "al-", the literal translation of "ahl bayt" is "household."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Into how many groups is Hadith classfied?
explanation:
Hadith is classified into two main groups:
1. Sahih (authentic): These are Hadith that are considered to be genuine and reliable, meeting the highest standards of authenticity.
2. Daif (weak): These are Hadith that are considered to be less reliable or authentic, due to issues with the chain of narrators or other factors.
However, some scholars also categorize Hadith into additional groups, such as:
- Hasan (good): These are Hadith that are considered to be reliable, but not to the same level as Sahih.
Mawdu' (fabricated): These are Hadith that are considered to be false or fabricated.
Maqlub (reversed): These are Hadith that have been altered or distorted in some way.
But the most common and basic classification is into two groups: Sahih and Daif.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of these hadith narrated the dialogue between the prophet (SAW) and angel Jibril?
explanation: Hadith 1 of An-Nawawi's collections narrates the dialogue between the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and angel Jibril, in which Jibril asks the Prophet to explain the meaning of Islam, Iman, and Ihsan. This Hadith is known as the "Hadith of Gabriel" and is considered a fundamental text in understanding the basics of Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The chain of narrators of hadith is called
explanation: The chain of narrators of a Hadith is called Isnad, which refers to the series of individuals who transmitted the Hadith from the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) to the present day. The Isnad is an essential part of the Hadith, as it allows scholars to verify the authenticity and reliability of the text.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Eid al-Adha is observed on the ___ day of dhul-Hijjah.
explanation: Eid al-Adha, also known as the Feast of Sacrifice, is celebrated on the tenth day of Dhul-Hijjah, marking the end of the Hajj pilgrimage.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul zalzalah stresses the
explanation:
Suratul Zalzalah, also known as Chapter 99 of the Quran, describes the events of the Day of Judgment, where the earth will shake and tremble, and humanity will be resurrected to face accountability for their deeds. The chapter emphasizes the power and majesty of Allah, and serves as a reminder of the ultimate reckoning that awaits all human beings.
The other options are not the primary focus of Suratul Zalzalah: day break refers to the beginning of a new day, day of Arafah is a specific day during the Hajj pilgrimage and night of power refers to Laylatul Qadr.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The main teaching of Hadith twelve of An-Nwawai's collections is
explanation: The main teaching of Hadith 12 of An-Nawawi's collections is the importance of non-interference in matters tha do not concern one. This Hadith teaches us to avoid meddling in the affairs of others and to focus on our own lives and responsibilities. It emphasizes the importance of respecting the autonomy and agency of others and nc interfering in their decisions or actions.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is not among the Ulul Azim? Prophet
explanation: Ulul Azim refers to the five most prominent prophets in Islam: Muhammad, Ibrahim, Musa, Nuh, and Idris. Whil Isa is a significant prophet in Islam, he is not included in this specific group.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are reporters of hadith except
explanation: Ulul Azim refers to the five most prominent prophets in Islam: Muhammad, Ibrahim, Musa, Nuh, and Idris. Whil Isa is a significant prophet in Islam, he is not included in this specific group.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following hadith discourages selfishness
explanation: Hadith 21 of An-Nawawi's collections discourages selfishness and emphasizes the importance of considering others. The Hadith states, "The merciful will be shown mercy by Ar-Rahman (Allah). Have mercy on those who are on earth, and the One in the heavens will have mercy on you." This Hadith teaches us to be compassionate and kind towards others, and to avoid being selfish and only thinking of our own interests.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al Qur an was compiled on the advice of
explanation:
- After the death of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), Abu Bakr received reports that some companions who had memorized the Quran had died in battles. He feared that the Quran might be lost if the remaining companions who memorized it also died.
- Abu Bakr consulted with Umar bin Khattab, and they decided to compile the Quran into a written book. -Zayd bin Thabit, who was a skilled writer and had previously written down revelations for the Prophet Muhammad, was tasked with compiling the Quran.
- Zayd gathered the written fragments of the Quran, verified them with the companions who had memorized the Quran, and compiled them into a single book.
Later, during the caliphate of Uthman bin Affan (d), the Quran was recompiled and standardized to ensure that there was only one official version of the Quran. This is why Uthman bin Affan is sometimes credited with compiling the Quran, but it was actually Zayd bin Thabit who did the initial compilation.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: ___ of An-Nawawi's collections warns against poke nosing.
explanation: An-Nawawi's collection of 40 Hadiths, also known as "Al-Arba'in", includes a Hadith that warns against poking one's nose into other people's affairs (Hadith number 6). This Hadith emphasizes the importance of minding one's own business and not interfering in the affairs of others. It teaches us to respect the privacy and boundarie of others and to focus on our own actions and responsibilities.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The preservation of al-Qur'an involves the following except
explanation: The preservation of the Quran involves several processes, including arrangement, compilation, recording, and standardization. However, italicization is not one of them. Italicization is a typographical style used to emphasize text, which was not relevant to the preservation of the Quran. The Quran was preserved through a rigorous process of memorization, writing, and verification, ensuring its authenticity and accuracy.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following were used in recording al Qur an at the time of the prophet (SAW) except
explanation:
During the time of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW), the Quran was recorded on various materials, including:
- Animal bones (a)
- Animal skin (b), also known as parchment
- Leaves (c), specifically palm leaves
Stone (e), specifically rocks or tablets.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ramadan is the ___ month of hijrah calendar.
explanation: Ramadan is the ninth month of the Islamic lunar calendar and is considered a sacred month, during which Muslims fast from dawn to sunset.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Ulul Azim are
explanation: As mentioned earlier, the five most prominent prophets in Islam are Muhammad, Ibrahim, Musa, Nuh, and Idris.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hasan means ___ Hadith
explanation: In the context of Hadith, "Hasan" refers to a Hadith that is considered "sound" or "good" in terms of its authenticity and reliability. A Hasan Hadith is one that has a strong chain of narration and is free from any defects or weaknesses. It is considered a high level of authenticity, second only to a Sahih Hadith, which is considered "authentic" or "sound".

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul Humazah consists of ___ verses
explanation: Suratul Humazah, also known as Surat Al-Humazah, is the 104th chapter of the Quran and consists of 7 verses. I is a Madinan surah that condemns those who slander and backbite others. The surah emphasizes the importance of guarding one's tongue and refraining from harming others through speech. It also warns against the consequences of spreading rumors and falsehoods. The 7 verses of Suratul Humazah provide guidance on the importance of responsible speech and the need to protect others from harm.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of these is the last chapter of al-Qur an?
explanation: The last two chapters of the Quran, An-Nas (114) and Al-Falaq (113), are short prayers for protection. The Qurai is divided into Surahs (chapters) and further divided into Ayahs (verses). The first surah in the Quran is Surah al Fatiha. Al-Qadr is the 97th chapter of the Quran. Al-Alaq is the 96th chapter of the Quran.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: How many times is the word al-Qari'ah?
explanation:
The word "al-Qari'ah" appears twice in Suratul-Qariah (Chapter 101 of the Quran): 1. In verse 1: "Al-Qari'ah (the striking Hour i.e. the Day of Resurrection)"
2. In verse 3: "And what will make you know what the striking (al-Qari'ah) is?"

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Laylatul-qadr means the night of
explanation: Laylatul Qadr, which is Arabic for "the Night of Power" or "the Night of Destiny", commemorates the night wher the Qur'an was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). It is considered a night of great spiritual significance, and is often translated as the Night of Power, Decree, or Destiny.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among these sahabah presided over compilation of Al-Qur'an?
explanation: During the caliphate of Abu Bakr, Zayd ibn Thabit was tasked with compiling the Quran into a single book. He was one of the scribes of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and was known for his expertise in the Quran. Zayd ibn Thabit was responsible for collecting the written fragments of the Quran and compiling them into a single manuscript. This manuscript became the standard text of the Quran and has been preserved and transmitted to this day.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Iyyaka na budu wa iyyaka nasta in is in suratul
explanation: The phrase "Iyyaka na budu wa iyyaka nasta'in" is a part of the first chapter of the Quran, Surat Al-Fatihah, whic is recited in every unit of prayer. This phrase is a declaration of worship and seeking help, and it means "You alone we worship and You alone we ask for help". Surat Al-Fatihah is considered the opening chapter of the Quran and sets the tone for the entire book. It emphasizes the unity and oneness of God, and the dependence of human beings on Him.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: There are ___ types of relevations.
explanation:
In Islamic theology, there are three types of revelations:
1. Wahy: This refers to the revelation of the Quran to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) through the angel Gabriel (Jibril).
2. Ilham: This refers to the inspiration or guidance given to the Prophet (SAW) or other righteous individuals, bu not in the form of a direct revelation like the Quran.
3. Ruya: This refers to dreams or visions that are believed to be from Allah, and may be a source of guidance or inspiration.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul adiyat warns man against his
explanation: Suratul Adiyat, also known as Chapter 100 of the Quran, warns humanity against ingratitude and neglect of Allah's blessings and favors. The chapter highlights the consequences of forgetting Allah's mercy and provision, and encourages humanity to be thankful and mindful of their responsibilities.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Allahus samad is translated as
options: and there is no one like Him
explanation:
Allah is He on whom all depend! "Allahus Samad" is a verse from Surat Al-Ikhlas (Chapter 112 of the Quran), anc
it is often translated as "Allah is He on whom all depend" or "Allah is the Eternal and Absolute".

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following hadith talks about the lawful and unlawful?
explanation:
Hadith 1 of An-Nawawi's collections talks about the lawful and unlawful. This Hadith is narrated by Umar ibn Al-Khattab and states:
"The lawful is clear, and the unlawful is clear, and between them are doubtful matters. Whoever leaves the doubtful matters for fear of falling into the unlawful will protect their religion and honor. And whoever indulges in the doubtful matters will fall into the unlawful, like a shepherd who grazes his sheep near a private pasture, and soon they will graze in it. Every king has a private pasture, and the private pasture of Allah is His unlawful matters." (Bukhari and Muslim).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are reporters of hadith except
explanation: Hadith 21 of An-Nawawi's collections discourages selfishness and emphasizes the importance of considering others. The Hadith states, "The merciful will be shown mercy by Ar-Rahman (Allah). Have mercy on those who are on earth, and the One in the heavens will have mercy on you." This Hadith teaches us to be compassionate and kind towards others, and to avoid being selfish and only thinking of our own interests.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul zalzalah stresses the
explanation:
Suratul Zalzalah, also known as Chapter 99 of the Quran, describes the events of the Day of Judgment, where the earth will shake and tremble, and humanity will be resurrected to face accountability for their deeds. The chapter emphasizes the power and majesty of Allah, and serves as a reminder of the ultimate reckoning that awaits all human beings.
The other options are not the primary focus of Suratul Zalzalah: day break refers to the beginning of a new day, day of Arafah is a specific day during the Hajj pilgrimage and night of power refers to Laylatul Qadr.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Actions are judged according to intention is part of hadith of An-Nawawi's collections.
explanation:
The Hadith "Actions are judged according to intention" is part of Hadith 1 of An-Nawawi's collections. This Hadith is considered one of the most important and foundational principles of Islamic ethics and jurisprudence. It emphasizes the importance of intention and sincerity in one's actions, and is often cited as a guiding principle for Muslims.
Here is the full text of the Hadith:
"Actions are judged according to intentions, and every person will be rewarded according to their intention. So, whoever migrates for the sake of Allah and His Messenger, their migration will be counted as such. And whoeve migrates for the sake of worldly gain or to marry a woman, their migration will be counted as such." (Bukhari
and Muslim)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul - Qariah consists of ___ verses
explanation:
Suratul-Qariah has eleven verses. Here are the verses and their English translations: 1. Al-Qari'ah (the striking Hour i.e. the Day of Resurrection)
2. What is the striking (Hour)?
3. And what will make you know what the striking (Hour) is?
4. It is a Day whereon mankind will be like moths scattered about
5. And the mountains will be like carded wool
6. Then as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be heavy
7. He will live a pleasant life (in Paradise)
8. But as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be light
9. He will have his home in Hawiyah (pit, i.e. Hell)
10. And what will make you know what it is?
11. It is a Fire, intensely hot

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Al Quran contains ___ chapters
explanation:
The Quran contains 114 chapters or surahs. Here are some facts about the chapters in the Quran: -The word "surah" is an Arabic term that denotes "chapter" in the Quran.
-The chapters in the Quran are not arranged in chronological order, and scholars have not been able to determine the exact order.
-The 114 chapters in the Quran are divided into verses, with the shortest chapter containing three verses and the longest containing 286 verses.
-The chapters in the Quran are classified as either Meccan or Medinan, depending on whether they were revealed before or after Muhammad's migration to Medina.
- The Meccan chapters generally deal with faith and scenes of the Hereafter, while the Medinan chapters are
more concerned with organizing the social life of the nascent Muslim community and leading Muslims to the ultimate goal of attaining.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Lakum Dinukum waliyadin is the last verse of suratul
explanation: The verse "Lakum Dinukum waliyadin" (meaning "To you, your religion, and to me, my religion") is indeed the last verse of Surat Al-Kafirun (Chapter 109 of the Qur'an). Well done! That's a great knowledge of the Qur'an!

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first revelation of al-Qur'an in suratul
explanation: The first revelation of the Qur'an was Surat Al-Alaq, which is Chapter 96 of the Qur'an. It was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) in the Cave of Hira, when he was 40 years old. This event marked the beginning of his prophethood and the revelation of the Qur'an.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah is the longest in the Qur'an?
explanation: Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest surah, consisting of 286 verses as seen throughout Qur'an 2.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who is known as "Kalimullah" (The one who spoke directly with Allah)?
explanation: Musa spoke directly with Allah, as confirmed in Qur'an 4:164: "And to Moses Allah spoke directly."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The event of Mi'raj is referenced in which Surah?
explanation:
The Mi'raj event is described in Qur'an 53:13-18: "And he certainly saw him in another descent, at the Lote Tree of the Utmost
Boundary."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which verse supports equality of all human beings?
explanation: Allah declares in Qur'an 49:13: "The most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet's people were turned into monkeys as punishment?
explanation: Some of Musa's people who violated the Sabbath were transformed into apes, as recorded in Qur'an 2:65: "Be you apes, despised."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was the wife of Pharaoh that believed in Allah?
explanation: Asiyah's faith is praised in Qur'an 66:11: "And Allah presents an example of those who believed: the wife of Pharaoh."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was commanded to sacrifice his son as a test from Allah?
explanation: Prophet Ibrahim was commanded to sacrifice his son Isma'il as a test of faith. (Qur'an 37:102).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The people of 'Ad were destroyed by
explanation:
The people of 'Ad were destroyed by a violent windstorm as mentioned in Qur'an 69:6: "And the people of Ad were destroyed by a
screaming, violent wind."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which angel brought revelations to Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
explanation: Angel Jibril (Gabriel) brought down the Qur'an to Prophet Muhammad. (Qur'an 2:97).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet's sons plotted against him?
explanation: The sons of Yaqub plotted against Yusuf, as mentioned in Qur'an 12:9: "Kill Joseph or cast him out to [some] land."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet received the Torah?
explanation: Musa was given the Torah as stated in Qur'an 5:44: "Indeed, We sent down the Torah, in which was guidance and light."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The blowing of the trumpet will be done by
explanation:
The trumpet will be blown by Israfil as described in Qur'an 39:68: "And the trumpet will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens
and whoever is on the earth will fall dead except whom Allah wills."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah contains Ayatul Kursi?
explanation: Ayatul Kursi is found in Qur'an 2:255: "Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of [all] existence."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the meaning of the name Al-Qur'an?
explanation: The word Qur'an means recitation, as indicated in Qur'an 75:17-18: "Indeed, upon Us is its collection and its recitation."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet could understand the language of the birds?
explanation:
Prophet Sulaiman was granted understanding of bird language, as stated in Qur'an 27:16: "O people, we have been taught the
language of birds."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet was swallowed by a big fish?
explanation: Yunus was swallowed by a big fish after abandoning his mission. (Qur'an 37:142).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which angel brought revelations to Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
explanation: Angel Jibril (Gabriel) brought down the Qur'an to Prophet Muhammad. (Qur'an 2:97).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The father of Prophet Yahya was
explanation:
Yahya's father was Zakariyyah, as mentioned in Qur'an 19:7: "O Zakariyyah, indeed We give you good tidings of a boy whose name
will be Yahya."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The covenant of all Prophets is referenced in which Surah?
explanation:
Allah mentions the covenant taken from all Prophets in Qur'an 33:7: "And [mention, O Muhammad], when We took from the
Prophets their covenant "

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first revealed verses of the Qur'an were in Surah
explanation: The first verses revealed were from Qur'an 96:1: "Recite in the name of your Lord who created."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet built the Ark by Allah's command?
explanation:
Allah instructed Prophet Nuh to build the Ark, as mentioned in Qur'an 11:37: "Construct the ship under Our observation and Our
inspiration."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The longest Ayah in the Qur'an is found in which Surah?
explanation:
The longest verse concerns debt and contracts, found in Qur'an 2:282: "O you who have believed, when you contract a debt for a
specified term, write it down."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Ka'bah was originally built by
explanation:
The Ka'bah was raised by Ibrahim and his son Isma'il as stated in Qur'an 2:127: "And [mention] when Abraham was raising the
foundations of the House and [with him] Ishmael."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The tribe of Quraysh is mentioned in Surah
explanation: The Quraysh are directly mentioned in Qur'an 106:1: "For the accustomed security of the Quraysh."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah states "There is no compulsion in religion"?
explanation: In Qur'an 2:256, Allah states: "There shall be no compulsion in [acceptance of] the religion."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The verse forbidding alcohol is found in Surah
explanation:
Alcohol prohibition is mentioned in Qur'an 5:90: "O you who have believed, indeed intoxicants, gambling, [sacrificing on] stone
alters, and divining arrows are but defilement from the work of Satan."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The name of the well near which Hajar found water is
explanation:
The well of Zamzam miraculously sprang up near Hajar and Isma'il in Makkah, indirectly referenced in Qur'an 14:37: "Our Lord, I
have settled some of my descendants in an uncultivated valley near Your sacred House."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah is called the Heart of the Qur'an?
explanation:
Surah Yasin is called the Heart of the Qur'an due to its comprehensive message, as emphasized in Hadith and referenced in Qur'an
36:1: "Ya, Seen."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet received the Zabur?
explanation: Prophet Dawud was given the Zabur, as mentioned in Qur'an 17:55: " ... And We gave to David the Zabur."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was thrown into the fire but saved by Allah?
explanation:
Prophet Ibrahim was cast into the fire by his people but was saved by Allah, as mentioned in Qur'an 21:69: "We said: O fire, be
coolness and safety upon Abraham."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who is said to blow the second trumpet to announce Resurrection?
explanation:
Israfil will blow the second trumpet, reviving the dead, as referenced in Qur'an 39:68: "Then it will be blown again, and at once
they will be standing, looking on."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What does Zakah literally mean?
explanation: Zakah purifies wealth and soul, as stated in Qur'an 9:103: "Take from their wealth a charity by which you purify them."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The people of Thamud were punished with
explanation:
The people of Thamud were struck by a thunderbolt, as stated in Qur'an 41:17: "So the thunderbolt seized them while they were
looking on."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah starts with "By the Fig and the Olive"?
explanation: This oath opens Qur'an 95:1: "By the fig and the olive."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Allah perfected the religion in which Surah?
explanation:
Allah declares the perfection of Islam in Qur'an 5:3: "This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon
you and have approved for you Islam as religion."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What was the occupation of Musa before prophethood?
explanation:
Musa worked as a shepherd before prophethood, as seen in Qur'an 28:23-25: "He found a group of men watering their flocks, and
aside from them he found two women holding back."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The splitting of the moon is mentioned in Surah
explanation: The miracle of the splitting of the moon is mentioned in Qur'an 54:1: "The Hour has drawn near, and the moon has split [in two]."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet spoke from the cradle?
explanation:
Prophet Isa spoke from the cradle to defend his mother, as recorded in Qur'an 19:29-30: "Indeed, I am the servant of Allah. He has
given me the Scripture."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the name of the surah that bears the name of Prophet Muhammad's family?
explanation:
Surah Al-Imran refers to the family of 'Imran, which includes Maryam (mother of Isa), as seen in Qur'an 3:33: "Indeed, Allah chose
Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham and the family of 'Imran."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was the first human created by Allah?
explanation: Allah created Adam as the first human being and appointed him as His vicegerent on earth. (Qur'an 2:30)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah is often recited for protection from evil?
explanation: Surah Al-Naas seeks protection from the whisperings of Satan (Qur'an 114:1-6).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet was tested with severe illness and remained patient?
explanation: Prophet Ayyub was afflicted with illness but remained patient (Qur'an 38:41-44).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet is associated with the building of Bayt al-Maqdis (Al-Aqsa)?
explanation: Prophet Sulaiman constructed the sanctuary (Qur'an 17:1 commentary).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the Hadith?
options: Sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad
explanation: Hadith refers to the Prophet's traditions, second only to the Qur'an in authority.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which companion of the Prophet was nicknamed 'As-Siddiq'?
explanation: Abu Bakr was called 'As-Siddiq' (the truthful) for affirming the Prophet's night journey (Isra').

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet was born without a father?
explanation: Isa was born to Maryam by Allah's command without a father (Qur'an 3:47).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah narrates the story of the People of the Cave?
explanation: The story of the sleepers is narrated in Qur'an 18.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In which Hijri year did the Prophet perform his Farewell Pilgrimage?
explanation: The Prophet performed Hajj in the 10th year after Hijrah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who killed Hamzah (RA) and later became a Muslim?
explanation: Wahshi killed Hamzah at Uhud but later embraced Islam and repented.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was the mother of Prophet Isa (Jesus)?
explanation: Maryam is the mother of Isa and is highly honoured in Qur'an 19:16-34.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was the Prophet given the Gospel (Injil)?
explanation: Allah gave Isa the Injil as mentioned in Qur'an 5:46.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the Arabic term for the pilgrimage to Makkah?
explanation: Hajj is the pilgrimage to the Ka'bah in Makkah, a pillar of Islam mentioned in Qur'an 3:97.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet was tested with the loss of all his children and wealth?
explanation: Prophet Ayyub showed exemplary patience during great personal loss (Qur'an 38:41).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What does the term "Huda" in the Qur'an mean?
explanation: "Huda" means guidance. The Qur'an is described as "a guidance for the righteous" (Qur'an 2:2).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Arabic term for permissible actions in Islam is:
explanation: Halal means lawful or permissible in Islamic jurisprudence.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet migrated from Makkah to which city?
explanation: The migration (Hijrah) was to Madinah, marking a new phase in Islam.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the Arabic term for voluntary fasting?
explanation: Nafl fasts are extra fasts performed beyond the obligatory ones.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: How many verses are in Surah Al-Fatihah?
explanation: Surah Al-Fatihah has seven verses and is recited in every unit of prayer.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Islamic calendar begins from the:
explanation: The Islamic (Hijri) calendar starts from the Prophet's migration to Madinah in 622 CE.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet is known for interpreting dreams?
explanation: Prophet Yusuf interpreted dreams in prison and for the king (Qur'an 12:43-49).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the Arabic term for the annual charity given by Muslims?
explanation: Zakah is an obligatory act of worship mentioned in Qur'an 9:60.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: How many compulsory prayers must a Muslim observe daily?
explanation: The five daily prayers are obligatory: Fajr, Zuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Caliph compiled the Qur'an into a single book form?
explanation: Caliph Uthman standardised and compiled the Qur'an (history of Uthmanic codex).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Qur'an was revealed over a period of how many years?
explanation: The Qur'an was revealed over 23 years-13 in Makkah and 10 in Madinah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Surah ends with the word "Mankind"?
explanation: Surah Al-Naas ends with "Min al-jinnati wan-naas" - "of jinn and mankind."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which surah is named after a woman?
explanation: "Al-Nisa" means "The Women" and addresses issues of women's rights and family law.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the meaning of 'La ilaha illa Allah'?
options: There is no deity except Allah
explanation: This is the declaration of tawhid, the foundation of Islamic belief.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet was gifted with the ability to control the jinn?
explanation: Sulaiman was given control over the jinn (Qur'an 27:17).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the Ka'bah also called in the Qur'an?
explanation: Qur'an 5:97 refers to the Ka'bah as "Al-Bayt al-Haram" - the Sacred House.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the name of the night the Qur'an was revealed?
explanation: Qur'an 97:1-5 refers to Laylat al-Qadr as the night of decree when the Qur'an was sent down.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the main theme of Surah Al-Ikhlas?
explanation: Surah Al-Ikhlas emphasises tawhid (monotheism) in Qur'an 112:1-4: 'Say: He is Allah, the One ... '

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the ruling on backbiting in Islam?
explanation: Difficulty Level: IntermediateBackbiting is condemned in Qur'an 49:12 as "eating the flesh of your dead brother."

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the name of the Prophet who cured the blind and lepers by Allah's permission?
explanation: Isa healed the blind and lepers by Allah's permission (Qur'an 3:49).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which prayer is offered just after sunset?
explanation: Maghrib is offered immediately after sunset and consists of three rak'ahs.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The reward for fasting is with whom?
explanation: According to Hadith Qudsi, "Fasting is Mine, and I will reward it" (Bukhari).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the ruling on praying without ablution (wudu) in Islam?
explanation: Purification is a condition for prayer (Qur'an 5:6).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who was the first martyr in Islam?
explanation: Sumayyah was killed for accepting Islam and is recognised as the first martyr.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which prayer is also called "Salat al-Wusta"?
explanation: Salat al-Wusta refers to the middle prayer, which is Asr (Qur'an 2:238).

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which Prophet is known for his wisdom and ability to judge fairly?
explanation: Prophet Dawud was known for fair judgment as referenced in Qur'an 38:20-22.
